site.title: byteflow.tools | Privacy-first Developer Tools
site.description: Privacy-first developer tools that run in your browser, with external-request tools clearly marked.
site.hero_badge: Open Source · Browser-local first · Always Free
site.hero_subtitle: Format, convert, generate, inspect — without sending data to servers. Your sensitive data stays in your browser.
site.hero_tagline: Most tools process locally. Always free. Open source.
site.popular_tools_subtitle: Start with these frequently used tools
site.explore_by_category_subtitle: Browse tools organized by use case

features.privacy_desc: Avoid pasting sensitive JWTs, API keys, and JSON payloads into opaque services. Most tools process in your browser; tools marked External request only use the network when you explicitly run them.
features.speed_desc: No spinners, no loading bars. Paste megabytes of text and watch it format, hash, or encode in real time.
features.keyboard_desc: {categoryCount} curated categories with visible tool counts help you jump to the right tool in seconds.
features.tools_desc: From JSONPath queries and Cron visualizers to QR generators and Chmod calculators. A unified, ever-expanding toolkit.

common.copy_failed: Unable to copy. Please copy manually.
common.download_blocked_until_checks_pass: Download is blocked until compliance checks pass.
common.generate_image_failed_retry: Unable to generate image. Please try again.
common.no_valid_image_files_loaded: No valid image files were loaded.
common.direct_download_blocked_opened_new_tab: Direct download was blocked by the remote host. Opened media in a new tab instead.
common.no_results_suggestion: Try another keyword, remove filters, or browse the workflow groups below.
common.family_data_formats: JSON, YAML, CSV, and data formats
common.thumbnail_status_idle: Paste a {platform} URL to extract thumbnail links.
common.thumbnail_status_invalid: Unable to parse a valid {platform} video ID.
common.thumbnail_status_unreachable: Could not verify candidate availability in-browser. Try another video URL.

external_network_notice.title: External network notice
external_network_notice.third_party_api_message: This tool may contact a third-party service from your browser as part of its workflow.
external_network_notice.user_requested_message: This tool may request or open a URL you provide from your browser when you explicitly run that action.
external_network_notice.user_input_preview_message: This tool may load external preview assets from your browser after you enter a supported URL.

purposes.authorized_media_download: Download media from a URL you provide after you confirm you are allowed to use it.
purposes.thumbnail_preview: Generate and preview public thumbnail image URLs derived from the video link you enter.

external_data.none: No tool input is sent by this feature.
external_data.user_provided_url: The URL you provide may be requested by your browser.
external_data.derived_url: A derived public asset URL may be requested by your browser.

external_network_notice.consent_required_message: Network access starts only after you choose the external-request action.

local_data_controls.title: Local data controls
local_data_controls.description: Byteflow processing stays in your browser. Some preferences and non-sensitive tool settings may be saved locally; sensitive payloads are configured not to persist by default.
local_data_controls.none_found_message: No saved Byteflow data was found.

privacy_badge.label: Browser-local
privacy_badge.title: Privacy-first
privacy_badge.description: This tool processes input in your browser. Tools marked External request disclose when a network call is needed.
privacy_badge.check1: No tool input upload for local tools

privacy_faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
privacy_faq.a1: For browser-local tools, input is processed in your browser and is not uploaded for processing. Tools marked External request disclose the network target and only contact it when you explicitly run that action.
privacy_faq.q2: How do I verify this tool is truly local?
privacy_faq.a2_step3: Use the tool with your data
privacy_faq.a2_step4: You may still see normal app assets, analytics page views, or service worker update checks. Tool input is processed locally unless the tool is marked External request and you run that action.
privacy_faq.a3: Many online tools upload data for processing. Byteflow keeps most workflows in the browser and labels tools that need an explicit external request, so you can decide before using sensitive data.

privacy_footer.privacy_text: Browser-local first; external requests are labeled

tool_trust_header.title: Tool trust status
tool_trust_header.browser_local_desc: Input and output are processed in this browser for this tool.
tool_trust_header.external_request_desc: This tool may contact the listed network target only when you run the external action.
tool_trust_header.offline_desc: Core processing works without network after the app has loaded.
tool_trust_header.network_required_desc: This workflow needs network for the external-request action.
tool_trust_header.sensitive_desc: Do not paste production secrets unless you have verified the local or disclosed network boundary.
tool_trust_header.standard_input_desc: No sensitive-input flag is set for this tool.
tool_trust_header.devtools_desc: Open the Network panel while using the tool to verify requests.
tool_trust_header.consent_label: Network access starts only after you choose the external-request action.

common.install_prompt_message: Install byteflow.tools for quicker access and offline usage.
common.install_inline_description: Install byteflow.tools for faster startup and offline access to browser-local tools. External-request tools still need network when you run their lookup actions.
common.not_found_description: The page you are looking for does not exist or was moved.
common.no_favorites: No favorites yet. Star tools from your recent list.
common.no_recent_tools: Open any tool to build your recent list.
common.cta_newsletter_desc: Star us on GitHub to get notified when we ship new developer tools.
common.route_error_title: Something went wrong on this page
common.route_error_description: An unexpected error occurred while rendering this route. You can try again safely.
common.drop_image_or_click_upload: Drop image or click to upload
common.headline: Headline
common.caption: Caption
common.thumbnail_public_only_notice: This tool only resolves public thumbnail image URLs. It does not download or process video/audio content.

hash_tool.input_and_secret_required: Input text and secret are both required.
hash_tool.enter_at_least_one_line: Enter at least one non-empty line.

common.deprecated_message: This tool has been deprecated as part of our focus on developer-first utilities. Consider using the suggested alternatives below.
common.local_input_too_large: Input is too large for local processing. Keep it under {size}.
common.local_row_limit_exceeded: Too many rows for local processing. Keep it under {count} rows.
common.local_results_truncated: Results truncated for performance. Showing first {count} items.

categories.formatters_desc: Format, beautify, and transform code and data files including JSON, XML, SQL, HTML, CSS, and YAML.
categories.text_string_desc: Encode, decode, hash, compare, and transform text and binary data.
categories.generators_desc: Generate UUIDs, passwords, timestamps, colors, QR codes, and placeholder content.
categories.network_web_desc: Test regex patterns, parse user agents, explore HTTP status codes, and debug web infrastructure.
categories.format_validate_desc: Format, validate, and inspect structured data and source code, including JSON, XML, SQL, HTML, and CSS.
categories.convert_encode_desc: Convert data across formats and encode/decode payloads such as Base64, URLs, HTML entities, and data files.
categories.text_content_desc: Refine text content with counting, cleanup, casing, diffing, and writing-focused helper utilities.
categories.web_api_desc: Debug web protocols and API flows with JWT, hashing, headers, HTTP, OpenAPI, and request builders.
categories.generators_ids_desc: Generate IDs, passwords, timestamps, secrets, and utility payloads for development and testing workflows.
categories.design_media_desc: Create visual and media assets such as code images, color outputs, SVG assets, and share-ready design snippets.
categories.data_code_formats_desc: Format, inspect, and convert JSON, YAML, XML, CSV, schemas, and code-oriented data formats.
categories.encoding_crypto_desc: Encode, decode, hash, inspect tokens, and work with certificate and key material locally.
categories.web_api_network_desc: Build, inspect, and debug HTTP, OpenAPI, URLs, user agents, headers, robots rules, and network data.
categories.devops_logs_desc: Parse environment files, scrub logs, inspect HAR files, build cron expressions, and prepare operations snippets.
categories.text_regex_desc: Clean, compare, count, transform, and test text, Unicode, slugs, regular expressions, and markdown content.
categories.images_svg_css_desc: Edit images, extract colors, optimize SVG, generate CSS visuals, and create local design assets.
categories.generators_calculators_desc: Generate IDs, passwords, timestamps, QR codes, barcodes, random lists, and test data.
categories.social_metadata_desc: Create open graph metadata, social previews, thumbnails, and share-ready social media assets.

json_formatter.tree_add_key_description: Provide a unique key name for this object.

base64_encode_decode.title: Base64 Encode/Decode
base64_encode_decode.description: Encode text to Base64 format or decode it back to a readable string.
base64_encode_decode.error_file_too_large: File is too large. Select a file smaller than 10MB and try again.
base64_encode_decode.error_file_required: Select a file before encoding in file mode.
base64_encode_decode.error_file_sample_unavailable: Select a file to try file encoding.
base64_encode_decode.error_encode_failed: Unable to encode input. Check the content and try again.
base64_encode_decode.error_invalid_base64: Invalid Base64 input. Check padding and characters, then retry.
base64_encode_decode.error_invalid_base64url: Invalid Base64URL input. Use URL-safe characters and try again.
base64_encode_decode.decode_binary_success: Binary decoded successfully ({bytes} bytes). Use Download to save the file.
base64_encode_decode.url_safe_hint: URL-safe Base64 uses - and _ instead of + and /, and may omit = padding.
base64_encode_decode.output_preview_truncated: Preview truncated. {hidden} more characters are hidden. Use Copy/Download for full output.
base64_encode_decode.input_placeholder_file: Paste Base64 content to decode binary...
base64_encode_decode.file_mode_hint: File mode uses the worker and never stores file contents.

url_encode_decode.title: URL Encode/Decode
url_encode_decode.description: Encode text for safe URL transmission or decode it back to readable string.
url_encode_decode.mode_component_desc: Encodes all special characters. Best for query values.
url_encode_decode.mode_reserved_desc: Preserves RFC3986 reserved delimiters and encodes everything else.
url_encode_decode.input_placeholder: Paste your text or URL-encoded string here...
url_encode_decode.encode_error: Unable to encode URL for this mode. Check the input and try again.
url_encode_decode.decode_error: Invalid URL-encoded string for current mode.
url_encode_decode.decode_error_non_hex: Percent escapes must use two hex digits.
url_encode_decode.decode_error_truncated: The percent escape is missing one or two hex digits.
url_encode_decode.decode_error_invalid_encoding: The percent bytes are not valid text for this decoder.
url_encode_decode.decode_error_fix: Replace the broken escape with %HH bytes, remove the stray percent sign, or switch to the mode that matches the source string.

jwt_decoder.title: JWT Decoder
jwt_decoder.description: Decode JSON Web Tokens instantly. Never sends your token to any server.
jwt_decoder.decode_only_title: Decode only — signature is not verified
jwt_decoder.decode_only_desc: This page only parses the JWT header and payload. A decoded token can still be forged, expired, or signed with the wrong key.
jwt_decoder.error_invalid_token: Invalid JWT format. Ensure token has 3 valid Base64URL segments.
jwt_decoder.error_segment_count: JWT must contain exactly 3 dot-separated segments: header, payload, and signature.
jwt_decoder.error_empty_segment: JWT header and payload segments cannot be empty.
jwt_decoder.error_invalid_base64url: One or more JWT segments are not valid Base64URL.
jwt_decoder.error_invalid_json: The decoded header or payload is not valid JSON.
jwt_decoder.error_decode_failed: Unable to decode token. Verify JWT format and try again.
jwt_decoder.claim_time_note: Time claims are Unix seconds. UTC is authoritative; local time is shown only as a convenience.
jwt_decoder.alg_none_warning: This token declares alg: none. Treat it as unsigned unless a verifier explicitly accepts that mode.
jwt_decoder.expired_warning: This token is expired according to its exp claim.
jwt_decoder.not_yet_valid_warning: This token is not valid yet according to its nbf claim.
jwt_decoder.issued_future_warning: This token has an iat claim in the future.
jwt_decoder.claim_detail_present: Claim is present; value is summarized to avoid repeating sensitive claim data.
jwt_decoder.claim_detail_invalid_timestamp: Claim is not a valid Unix timestamp.
jwt_decoder.no_registered_claims: No exp, nbf, iat, iss, or aud claims were found.

hash_generator.title: Hash Generator
hash_generator.description: Instantly generate MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, and SHA-512 hashes from text.
hash_generator.sha1_warning: SHA-1 is considered weak for security use. Prefer SHA-256 or stronger.
hash_generator.batch_empty: Enter one item per line to generate batch hashes.
hash_generator.hmac_secret_hint: HMAC secrets stay in memory only and are not saved.
hash_generator.file_privacy_hint: File bytes stay in memory and are cleared when you reset or leave file mode.

text_diff_checker.title: Text Diff Checker
text_diff_checker.description: Compare two pieces of text side-by-side. Highlights additions, deletions, and modifications instantly.

env_parser.title: Env Variable Parser
env_parser.description: Parse .env files, validate variables, and export to JSON, YAML, or Docker args.
env_parser.sample_input:
# Application Config
NODE_ENV=production
PORT=3000
HOST=0.0.0.0

# Database
DATABASE_URL="postgres://user:pass@localhost:5432/mydb"
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379

# Authentication
JWT_SECRET=super-secret-jwt-key-2024
JWT_EXPIRES_IN=7d
SESSION_COOKIE_NAME=__session

# Third-Party API Keys
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_live_51abc123
SENDGRID_API_KEY=SG.xxxxx.yyyyy
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY
AWS_REGION=us-east-1

# Feature Flags
ENABLE_ANALYTICS=true
ENABLE_RATE_LIMITING=false
DEBUG=false

csv_json_converter.error_json_array_required: JSON must be an array of objects or arrays.

id_generator.title: ID Generator
id_generator.description: Generate UUID v4, UUID v7, ULID, and NanoID in bulk with configurable options.
id_generator.about_uuid_v4: UUID v4 uses 122 random bits, so collisions are vanishingly unlikely.
id_generator.about_uuid_v7: UUID v7 embeds a Unix millisecond timestamp, which keeps identifiers sortable by creation time.
id_generator.about_ulid: ULID combines a 48-bit timestamp prefix with Crockford Base32 encoding for lexicographically sortable IDs.
id_generator.about_nanoid: NanoID generates compact, URL-friendly unique IDs. The default 21-character format provides about 126 bits of entropy.

certificate_decoder.description: Decode PEM-encoded X.509 certificates and inspect all details locally.

certificate_decoder.validity_invalid: Certificate has expired or is not yet valid

slugify_case_converter.description: Convert text between slug, camelCase, PascalCase, snake_case, and more.
slugify_case_converter.input_placeholder: Enter text, variable name, or sentence...
slugify_case_converter.acronym_hint: When enabled, uppercase acronyms remain uppercase in camel/pascal/title outputs.

slugify_case_converter.score_empty: Enter text to generate a slug score and diagnostics.
slugify_case_converter.warning_empty_slug: Slug is empty. Add letters or numbers first.
slugify_case_converter.warning_too_short: Slug is short ({length} chars). Add meaningful keywords.

invisible_chars_detector.option_remove_zero_width: Remove zero-width characters (ZWSP, ZWNJ, ZWJ, BOM)
invisible_chars_detector.option_normalize_spaces: Normalize spaces (NBSP, ideographic space → regular space)

pages.about_intro: A free, open-source collection of developer tools where most workflows run in your browser. Tools that need external requests are labeled before network access.
pages.pricing_intro: Byteflow's browser-local tools are free to use, self-host, and contribute to. There are no accounts, premium tiers, cloud history, team workspaces, or server-side tool processing.
pages.pricing_free_plan_desc: Use every public tool with no signup, uploads, or paywalls.
pages.pricing_pro_plan_desc: Features are not gated behind Pro plans or paid upgrades.
pages.pricing_team_plan_desc: Byteflow does not offer hosted accounts, cloud history, team workspaces, or tool APIs in this open-source app.
pages.pricing_feature_fast_updates: Frequent tool updates and new launches
pages.about_privacy_desc: Most processing happens locally in your browser. Tools marked External request disclose when they contact the network.
pages.about_fast_desc: No account and no tool-processing backend. Browser-local tools can keep working offline after the app shell is cached.
pages.about_oss_desc: Built with Next.js, TypeScript, and Shadcn UI. Contributions welcome on GitHub.
pages.privacy_no_collection_desc: Most byteflow.tools workflows process input and output in your browser. We do not collect, store, analyze, or upload browser-local tool payloads. Tools marked External request may contact the listed network target only when you explicitly run that action.
pages.privacy_cookies_desc: We use localStorage only for safe UI preferences such as theme, language, sidebar state, and local tool settings. Sensitive payloads, tokens, logs, file contents, and tool outputs are not meant to be stored there.
pages.privacy_analytics_desc: We may use cookie-free Cloudflare Web Analytics to understand aggregate page views. We do not track tool inputs, outputs, or personal data through analytics.
pages.privacy_third_party_desc: Some tools may request external APIs or user-provided URLs to provide functionality. Those tools are labeled External request and describe the network purpose before you run them.
pages.privacy_contact_desc: For privacy-related questions, open an issue on our GitHub repository or contact us via the Contact page.
pages.terms_acceptance_desc: By using byteflow.tools, you agree to these terms. All tools are provided free of charge and as-is.
pages.terms_use_desc: You may use byteflow.tools for personal and commercial purposes. Browser-local tools process data locally; tools marked External request may contact the listed network target when you run them. You are responsible for the data you process.
pages.terms_disclaimer_desc: Tools are provided "as is" without warranty of any kind. We do not guarantee the accuracy, completeness, or reliability of any tool output. Use at your own risk.
pages.terms_liability_desc: In no event shall byteflow.tools be liable for any damages arising from the use or inability to use our tools, including data loss or security incidents.
pages.terms_changes_desc: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated date.
pages.contact_intro: We'd love to hear from you. Choose your preferred way to reach us.
pages.contact_github_desc: Report bugs, request features, or contribute code.
pages.contact_issues_desc: Open a GitHub issue for bug reports or feature requests.
pages.contact_email_desc: For business inquiries or partnership opportunities.
pages.privacy_external_request_tools_desc: This list is generated from tool manifests. These tools may contact the listed domains only for the disclosed purpose and only after the external-request action is chosen.
pages.trust_center_intro: Understand how byteflow.tools labels browser-local tools, external requests, sensitive inputs, storage, analytics, PWA cache behavior, security headers, and vulnerability reporting.
pages.trust_center_summary_local_desc: Most tools process input and output in your browser. External-request tools are labeled before network access.
pages.trust_center_summary_network_desc: External request tools disclose domains, purpose, and the kind of data your browser may send.
pages.trust_center_summary_security_desc: The app uses explicit trust labels, CSP-aware rendering, and a public vulnerability reporting path.
pages.trust_center_browser_local_desc: The tool runtime transforms input and output in the current browser tab without a tool-processing backend.
pages.trust_center_offline_desc: After the app shell and tool chunks have loaded, the core workflow can continue without network access when this label is present.
pages.trust_center_external_request_desc: The tool can contact a disclosed network target only for the listed purpose and only when you run that external action.
pages.trust_center_sensitive_input_desc: The tool commonly handles tokens, keys, logs, certificates, URLs, or files, so verify the runtime boundary before pasting production data.
pages.trust_center_verify_intro: Use your browser Network panel to confirm whether a tool stays local or starts the disclosed external request.
pages.trust_center_verify_step1: Open the tool page, then open DevTools and select the Network panel.
pages.trust_center_verify_step2: Enable Preserve log, clear the request list, and keep the panel open while using the tool.
pages.trust_center_verify_step3: Run the tool with sample input. Browser-local tools should not send your input or output to a tool-processing endpoint.
pages.trust_center_verify_step4: For tools marked External request, confirm the request only appears after your explicit action and targets the listed domain.
pages.trust_center_external_tools_desc: This table is generated from tool manifests so the Trust Center, privacy policy, and tool badges stay aligned.
pages.trust_center_storage_desc: Local storage is reserved for safe preferences such as theme, language, sidebar state, recent tool IDs, and local presets. Tool payloads, tokens, logs, file contents, and generated outputs must not be persisted by default.
pages.trust_center_analytics_desc: Analytics are limited to aggregate product signals such as page views and safe event names. Tool input, output, JWTs, secrets, log bodies, file contents, image contents, search query text, and full URLs are not analytics fields.
pages.trust_center_pwa_desc: The PWA may cache the app shell, static assets, icons, and tool chunks for offline use. It must not cache tool input, tool output, uploaded file content, or external-request responses.
pages.trust_center_security_headers_desc: Security header checks guard transport security, referrer policy, content type sniffing, permissions policy, frame ancestors, object sources, and a CSP that avoids arbitrary script sources.
pages.trust_center_xss_desc: Tools that preview Markdown, HTML, SVG, or metadata should sanitize user-controlled markup and avoid relaxing CSP to make previews work.
pages.trust_center_vulnerability_desc: Report suspected vulnerabilities through GitHub Security Advisories or the repository issue process. Do not include production secrets or private payloads in public reports.
pages.trust_center_faq_q1: Do all tools run completely offline?
pages.trust_center_faq_a1: No. Browser-local tools can continue offline after assets are cached, but tools marked External request need network access for the disclosed action.
pages.trust_center_faq_q2: Does byteflow.tools store tool input or output?
pages.trust_center_faq_a2: Tool payloads are not meant to be stored by default. Storage is limited to safe preferences and local presets that must not contain sensitive payloads.
pages.trust_center_faq_q3: How do I know an external request is expected?
pages.trust_center_faq_a3: Check the tool trust header and this generated external request table, then verify in DevTools that network access starts only after your explicit action.
